title: grammitis eximia
intro: Grammitis eximia, also known as the eximious polypody fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in shaded, humid areas. Traditionally, Grammitis eximia has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
